MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN OPPOS TO DEFT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDMGENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF SWANSON, ALBERT G

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS DR. FREDERICK BOST, CALIFORNIA PACIFIC ORTHOPAEDICS & SPORTS MEDICINE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTS THE BURDEN OF PROOF WITH DR. SAMPSON'S DECLARATION. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. CAUSATION MUST BE PROVEN WITHIN A REASONABLE MEDICAL PROBABILITY BASED UPON COMPETENT EXPERT TESTIMONY. MERE POSSIBILITY ALONE IS INSUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH A PRIMA FACIE CASE. SEE JONES V. ORTHO PHARMACEUTICAL CORP. (1985) 163 CAL.APP.3D 396. PLAINTIFF'S EXPERT DECLARATIONS FROM DOCTORS SLATER AND SMITH DO NOT MEET THIS STANDARD. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; REPORTER: JOANNE FARRELL, CSR #4838

REPLY BRIEF TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGEMNT/ADJUDICATION FILED BY DEFENDANT PRESIDIO SURGERY CENTER LLC

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT PRESIDIO SURGERY CENTER, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED. DEFENDANT PSC SHIFTS THE BURDEN OF PROOF WITH THE DECLARATION OF EXPERT WEBB SUPPORTED BY ATTACHED AND CERTIFIED MEDICAL RECORDS. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT THAT DEFENDANT PSC COMMITTED ANY NEGLIGENCE OR BREACHED A DUTY TO PROVIDE A SAFE ENVIRONMENT FOR MEDICAL CARE. THE STATEMENT OF PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGED EXPERT CONTAINED IN PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION IS INADMISSIBLE HEARSAY. IN ADDITION, THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED OPPOSITION OF 07/29/10 AS IT WAS UNTIMELY AND THEREFORE STRICKEN. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR CONTINUANCE DENIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Albert Swanson (902.5920) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona; Caryn A. Bordonaro of the Law Offices of Steven D. Hillyard (334.6880) for defendants and moving parties

Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS DR. FREDERICK BOST, CALIFORNIA PACIFIC ORTHOPAEDICS & SPORTS MEDICINE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifts the burden of proof with Dr. Sampson's declaration. Plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue of fact. Causation must be proven within a reasonable medical probability based upon competent expert testimony. Mere possibility alone is insufficient to establish a prima facie case. See Jones v. Ortho Pharmaceutical Corp. (1985) 163 Cal.App.3d 396. Plaintiff's expert declarations from doctors Slater and Smith do not meet this standard. Order and judgment signed in open court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Reporter: Joanne Farrell, CSR #4838

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-30-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT PRESIDIO SURGERY CENTER, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Albert G. Swanson, 902-5920 pltf & opposing party in propria persona (did not give proper notice); Benjamin Ikuta, Esq. of Low McKinley Baleria Weber & Salenko, LLP 916/231-2400 for deft & moving party.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANTED. DEFENDANT PSC SHIFTS THE BURDEN OF PROOF WITH THE DECLARATION OF EXPERT WEBB SUPPORTED BY ATTACHED AND CERTIFIED MEDICAL RECORDS. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT THAT DEFENDANT PSC COMMITTED ANY NEGLIGENCE OR BREACHED A DUTY TO PROVIDE A SAFE ENVIRONMENT FOR MEDICAL CARE. THE STATEMENT OF PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGED EXPERT CONTAINED IN PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION IS INADMISSIBLE HEARSAY. In addition, the Court did not consider plaintiff's amended opposition of 07/29/10 as it was untimely and therefore stricken. Plaintiff's request for continuance denied. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice And Motion To Compel Further Responsesto Form Interrogatories, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Production Of Documents Set One Pursuant To Court Order And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-24-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT PRESIDIO SURGERY CENTER LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One; And Request For Production, Set One, Pursuant To Court Order is granted. No opposition has been filed. Plaintiff's failure to respond fully in compliance with the court's prior order was a misuse of the discovery process. Plaintiff is ordered to respond further within 15 days of notice of this order. Monetary sanctions are awarded in the amount of $577.50, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Evidentiary sanctions are granted as Plaintiff is barred from offering into evidence any document that has not been produced to Defendant by June 21, 2010. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.
